description_long: This helicopter features a lightweight, open-frame tail boom and a rounded cockpit with large, clear windows, providing excellent visibility for the pilot and passengers. The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades with yellow tips, while the tail rotor is mounted at the end of the tail boom and is protected by a simple frame. The landing skids are tubular and metallic, extending parallel to the fuselage with crossbars for stability. The cockpit is enclosed with a bubble canopy, and the fuselage appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and transparent materials. The helicopter's design is reminiscent of early utility or training helicopters, with exposed structural elements and minimal body panels. No visible registration numbers or markings are present on the exterior.
description: A lightweight helicopter with a bubble cockpit, exposed tail boom, and two-blade main rotor. Features metallic landing skids and large, clear cockpit windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bubble cockpit, exposed tail boom, two-blade main rotor, and metallic landing skids.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the rounded cockpit canopy, wide rotor span, and parallel landing skids.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cockpit, open tail structure, and rotor configuration.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail boom, tail rotor, and the alignment of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, with yellow tips and a straight, slightly tapered shape.
attribute_tail_rotor: Present at the end of the tail boom, appears to have two blades oriented vertically, with a protective frame around it and no visible markings.
attribute_landing_skids: Tubular metallic skids, silver-gray in color, running parallel to the fuselage with crossbars for support and stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Large, rounded bubble canopy forming the cockpit windows, made of clear glass or acrylic, with minimal framing and no visible markings.
